# Irori

> Irori is an open-source, local-first desktop AI companion app built around character cards, local memory, OpenAI-compatible model settings, and tool safety gates.

Irori turns agent sessions into character-driven desktop companions for thinking, writing, coding, planning, and follow-through. Each companion can have its own character card, voice, avatar, prompt fragments, memory surface, and workflow role.

## Primary Links

- Source code: https://github.com/hikariming/irori
- Latest release: https://github.com/hikariming/irori/releases/latest
- License: Apache-2.0

## Key Facts

- Product category: local-first desktop AI companion app
- Core audience: developers, AI tool users, character-card creators, and users who want local control over AI companions
- Main features: character cards, local memory, model provider presets, OpenAI-compatible configuration, tool safety gates
- Desktop stack: Tauri and React
- Runtime focus: local companion workspace with configurable model providers

## Suggested Descriptions

- Short: Irori is a local-first desktop AI companion app with character cards, local memory, model presets, and tool safety gates.
- Long: Irori is an open-source desktop app that turns AI agent sessions into character-driven companions. It supports editable character cards, local memory, OpenAI-compatible model providers, and safety confirmation flows for local tool use.
